summary.brief: Hayward v. Summa Health Sys.. Medical malpractice case involving neurology.

evidence_index.text: Following bouts of diverticulitis, Ms. Hayward elected to have a portion of her sigmoid colon removed in an attempt to remedy the problem.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On October 10, 2007, Defendant-Appellee Dr. Michael Cullado, M.D., and Defendant-Appellee Dr. Steven Wanek, M.D., a fifth-year surgical resident employed by Defendant-Appellee Summa Health System (“Summa”), performed the partial colectomy on Ms. Hayward.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Hayward developed weakness and loss of sensation in her left leg. Following a neurology consult by Dr. Robert Lada, M.D., Dr. Lada determined that Ms. Hayward suffered a nerve injury to the left femoral nerve during the surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Lada concluded that the injury occurred due to a prolonged compression of the nerve during surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Robert Lada, M.D.

evidence_index.text: Ms. Hayward’s expert, Dr. William Irvin, M.D., testified that Drs. Cullado and Wanek fell “below the accepted standards of care * * * []” and that doing so resulted in Ms. Hayward’s neuropathy.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. William Irvin, M.D.

evidence_index.text: The cause of [Ms. Hayward’s] injury came from compression of the femoral nerve with a lateral retractor blade[]” that was inappropriately placed.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. William Irvin, M.D.

evidence_index.text: Dr. Peter Muscarella II, M.D., testified that he did not believe the surgeons “deviated from the standard of care.”
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Peter Muscarella II, M.D.

evidence_index.text: The jury concluded that Dr. Cullado and Summa were not liable, that Dr. Cullado and Summa by and through Dr. Wanek were not negligent in the care and treatment of Ms. Hayward, and that they did not cause injury to Ms. Hayward.
evidence_index.speaker: Jury verdict

evidence_index.text: Viewing the evidence in a light most favorable to the Defendants, and without evaluating credibility, there was evidence by which a jury could have concluded that the Defendants were not liable.
evidence_index.speaker: Court analysis

evidence_index.text: We affirm the Summit County Court of Common Pleas’ ruling on Ms. Hayward’s motion for a JNOV, reverse the jury verdicts, and remand the matter for a new trial.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Following bouts of diverticulitis, Ms. Hayward elected to have a portion of her sigmoid colon removed.

requestables.name: Neurology Consult
requestables.description: Consultation to assess weakness and loss of sensation in left leg

reveal.result_summary: Nerve injury to the left femoral nerve due to prolonged compression during surgery
reveal.clinical_significance: Identified cause of postoperative leg weakness

timeline_phases.name: Preoperative Assessment
timeline_phases.description: Patient evaluated for elective surgery due to diverticulitis

key_events.event: Decision to proceed with partial colectomy

timeline_phases.name: Postoperative Recovery
timeline_phases.description: Patient develops weakness and loss of sensation in left leg

key_events.event: Neurology consult identifies femoral nerve injury

decision_points.prompt: How should the retractor be placed to avoid nerve injury?

options.description: Place retractor carefully to avoid nerve compression
options.clinical_reasoning: Proper placement minimizes risk of nerve injury

options.description: Place retractor without specific attention to nerve compression
options.clinical_reasoning: Inappropriate placement can lead to nerve injury

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant placed retractor leading to nerve compression

expected_action_court.description: Retractor should have been placed to avoid nerve compression
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated improper placement caused injury

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Improper retractor placement led to nerve injury
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Retractor should have been placed with care to avoid nerve compression
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed permanent weakness and loss of sensation in left leg
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Requires assistance for mobility, unlikely to improve

key_findings: Jury found no deviation from standard of care
Court affirmed no liability despite evidence of retractor injury

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper placement could have avoided nerve injury

factual_timeline.event: Partial colectomy performed by Dr. Cullado and Dr. Wanek

allegations.allegation: Improper placement of retractor causing nerve injury

court_findings.finding: No deviation from standard of care
